{"key_event":"Mass mobilization amid renewed armed territorial control","reasoning":"Civil society strengthened modestly as national protests, youth/feminist/ethnic mobilization, elections, courts, Congress, and local governments constrained executive power, though killings of activists and armed intimidation limited autonomy in many regions. State power declined from its prior high because armed groups and illegal economies expanded in peripheral territories and reform capacity was fragmented, despite continued national administrative capacity, migration regularization, security operations, and peaceful democratic alternation.","society_change":0.4,"society_power":6.7,"state_change":-0.6,"state_power":6.6}
